evals.prompt: I want to see how many links were created each month for the past year. Can you generate a chart?
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart saved to ./reports/links-per-month.png showing 12 months of link creation data with labeled bars, month labels on x-axis, and link counts on y-axis.
expectations: The script installs required Python packages (psycopg2-binary, matplotlib, python-dotenv)
The script is executed from the skill's scripts/ directory
A PNG file is created at ./reports/links-per-month.png
The chart has 12 bars (one per month)

evals.prompt: Generate a link creation stats chart and save it to ./analytics/monthly-links.png
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart saved to ./analytics/monthly-links.png (custom path) showing 12 months of data.
expectations: The --output flag is used to specify ./analytics/monthly-links.png
A PNG file is created at the custom path ./analytics/monthly-links.png
The analytics/ directory is created if it doesn't exist
The chart content is the same as the default (12 months, labeled bars)

evals.prompt: Show me the monthly link trends from the database as a bar chart image
evals.expected_output: A PNG bar chart saved to the default location showing monthly link creation trends for the past 12 months.
expectations: The skill is triggered even though the user didn't say 'generate_chart.py' or 'link-stats-chart'
The script runs successfully and produces a PNG
The chart title references 'Links Created Per Month'
All 12 months are represented on the x-axis even if some have zero links
